basics.name: Kirill Demidov
basics.label: Data Engineer Team Lead
basics.summary: Experienced Data Engineer with expertise in BI, analytics, and large-scale data systems. Proficient in cloud data platforms, pipeline orchestration, and leading engineering teams to build scalable data solutions.

work.name: OneUpGames (formerly Guns and Gangs)
work.summary: Leading the data engineering team, building modular automation tools, and developing complex product analytics for mobile games using GCP, BigQuery, and Dataform.
highlights: Developed 'GG DE Bot': A modular Slack bot for Data Engineering automation with AI-driven BigQuery metadata management (OpenAI) and automated PII masking.
Designed and implemented complex product analytics pipelines in Dataform for mobile gaming behavior, battle metrics, and tutorial funnels.
Architected a centralized data governance system including regression alerting and automated BigQuery documentation.
Modernized data infrastructure and CI/CD workflows using GCP Cloud Run, Cloud Build, and Artifact Registry.

work.name: Arpeely
work.summary: Leading the data engineering team, building scalable data infrastructure, and developing BI solutions using Google Cloud, BigQuery, and Dataform.
highlights: Refactored ELT pipelines from custom Python to scalable Dataform solution.
Designed and implemented a corporate Data Warehouse and Data Lake in GCP.
Deployed Looker-based BI system from scratch.
Implemented CI/CD pipelines for DWH and reporting.
Developed a data governance monitoring system.

work.name: Tango.me
work.summary: Managed a team of data engineers and BI developers, implementing data pipelines and analytics solutions in GCP.
highlights: Led a team of 6 data engineers and BI developers.
Developed corporate Data Lake and Data Warehouse in GCP with Airflow and dbt.
Implemented data governance and data quality monitoring using SelectStar and SodaSQL.
Designed and built analytics and operational reports on Looker.

work.name: Alterosmart Solutions
work.description: Big Data solutions provider specializing in IIoT and analytics.
work.summary: Led the Big Data team, designing and implementing data pipelines, data warehouses, and analytics solutions for industrial IoT systems.
highlights: Analyzed business requirements, designed, and implemented Big Data modules in PDC systems.
Developed and optimized ETL/ELT processes for IIoT systems (PDC, BMS) using Apache NiFi, Data Build Tool, and Spark.
Designed and implemented an operational Data Warehouse for PDC application data using PostgreSQL.
Built a Data Mart module for Atlassian Jira Software based on PostgreSQL and Cube.js.
Developed and visualized analytical reports for DF data processing.

work.name: SafeCharge
work.summary: Designed and developed BI solutions, Data Marts, and reporting systems, while maintaining ETL processes for internal analytics.
highlights: Analyzed business requirements and designed Data Marts for internal clients.
Maintained an ETL platform based on SSIS.
Developed a semantic layer using SSAS (OLAP Cube).
Designed and implemented a reporting system using SSRS and Power BI.
Migrated OLAP-based BI system to a Big Data environment using AWS Data Lake (S3), ELT pipelines with Apache NiFi and Spark (PySpark), and MemSQL as the semantic layer.

work.name: Data Cube
work.description: BI and Big Data consultancy serving enterprise clients.
work.position: BI Project Manager / BI & Big Data Engineer
work.summary: Managed BI projects and developed BI & Big Data solutions for various enterprises.
highlights: Managed BI projects from conceptualization to full implementation.
Analyzed business requirements and developed project deliverables.
Maintained ETL platforms using SSIS, Python, and T-SQL.
Designed and implemented Data Marts and Data Warehouses for internal analytics systems.
Developed OLAP and Tabular Cube semantic layers.
Implemented reporting systems using Pyramid BI Office, SSRS, Necto Panorama, Power BI, and Business Objects.

Served major clients including BioCatch, Postal Bank of Israel, Bituach Yashir, Mimun Yashir, MKS Instruments, Taglit Birthright Israel, Panaya, Reuth Medical Rehabilitation Center, Medinol, LiderForex, and Amdocs.

work.name: Company for Location and Restitution of Holocaust Victims' Assets
work.description: A governmental organization dedicated to identifying and restituting assets to Holocaust survivors and their heirs.
work.position: Head of Data Management and Data Integration Department
work.summary: Led data management initiatives, developed data integration tools, and managed ETL and reporting systems.
highlights: Developed knowledge management tools using MS SQL Server 2012.
Designed and managed ETL processes for data integration and reporting.
Created, managed, and supported a SharePoint-based database system.
Led a team in developing research projects, providing training, and knowledge sharing.

projects.name: SOZD Legislative Monitoring ecosystem
projects.description: Built a multi-layer ecosystem for monitoring and analyzing Russian legislative data using LLMs and semantic search.
highlights: Implemented a RAG-based semantic search system over government transcripts using Qdrant and OpenAI embeddings.
Developed an LLM-powered analysis service for bills using Google Gemini (1.5 Pro/Flash).
Built a centralized RestProxy API (FastAPI) as a gateway to PostgreSQL and BigQuery.
Created multiple interfaces: analytics web portal (Flask/Bootstrap) and real-time Telegram monitoring bots.

projects.name: GCP Billing CLI Tool
projects.description: Terminal-based tool for rapid analysis and optimization of GCP costs.
highlights: Provides deep dives into Compute Engine, BigQuery, and Networking costs from the CLI.
Integrates with Google Recommender to suggest cost-saving opportunities.
Containerized version for consistent deployment across DE teams.

projects.name: Data Lake Implementation
projects.description: Designed and built a corporate Data Lake for analytics and reporting.
highlights: Implemented scalable cloud storage solutions.
Optimized data partitioning and query performance.
Automated data ingestion using Apache Airflow.
